english: pf. bstiṅs (bstings), fut. bstiṅ (bsting), imp. stiṅs (stings), to rebuke, scold, abuse Lexx.

pf. bstims (bstims), fut. bstim (bstim), imp. stims (stims), prop. vb. causative to °tim-pa ('thim pa), gen. = °tim-pa ('thim pa), to enter, penetrate, pervade, to be absorbed in, thugs čos-ñyid-kyi kloṅ-du stim (thugs chos nyid kyi klong du stim) Pth. the soul is absorbed in the expanse of the čos-ñyid (chos nyid).
cunnus, orifice of the vagina, the vulg. and obscene expression for the pudendum muliebre.

1. abstract noun and adj., thickness, density, thick; stugs-po (stugs po) adj., = °tug-pa ('thug pa), °tug-po ('thug po), thick, dense, e.g. a forest, Dzl.; sound, heavy (sleep, clouds etc.); dpal-stugs (dpal stugs) right noble, most noble Cs.; stugs-po-bkod-pa (stugs po bkod pa) Pth. one of the heavens of Buddha. -- 2. a wind, flatulence C.
pf. bstuṅs (bstungs), fut. bstuṅ (bstung), imp. stuṅs (stungs) = rtuṅ-ba (rtung ba).

pf. and fut. bstud (bstud), to repeat, to reiterate, to give or offer repeatedly (medicine, food, beer etc.), bstud-na (bstud na) if it is repeated Mñg.; sbrid-pa maṅ-po stud-čiṅ °oṅ (sbrid pa mang po stud ching 'ong) repeated sneezing ensues Lt.; bstud-nas na-ba (bstud nas na ba) to be always ill Sch.; cf. btud-mar (btud mar).

pf. and fut. bstun (bstun), prop. causative to °tun-pa ('thun pa), gen. = °tun-pa ('thun pa), to agree, dge-ba bču-la bstun-pai rgyal-krims (dge ba bcu la bstun pai rgyal khrims) a law agreeing with the ten virtues Glr.; °dod-yon lṅa daṅ stun-pai loṅs-spyod ('dod yon lnga dang stun pai longs spyod) a life of pleasure in accordance with the five enjoyments Glr.; dus-skabs daṅ stun-te (dus skabs dang stun te) agreeably to the (proper) time, in due time Glr.; ṅai žiṅ rmo-ba °di daṅ stun-pai mgur-ma (ngai zhing rmo ba 'di dang stun pai mgur ma) a song having reference to this my labour in the fields Mil.; gžuṅ daṅ stun-pa (gzhung dang stun pa) Lex., Cs.: 'to confer, to make agree with the original text'.
or ste-pa (ste pa), Ld., for btub-pa (btub pa), gtub-pa (gtub pa).

an affix for the gerund, inst. of te (te), after g (g), ṅ (ng), and vowels, v. te (te). -- As ste (ste) contains the copula, it may be added also to other words than verbs, e.g. kyod rigs če-žiṅ mto-ba-ste (khyod rigs che zhing mtho ba ste) as you are of high and noble extraction Dzl.; like °di-lta-ste ('di lta ste) it is also used for namely, to wit, videlicet (viz.), that is to say, esp. before translations of foreign words and names: śi-ra-ste ṅgo-bo žes-bya-ba (shi ra ste ngo bo zhes bya ba) Tar. 11, 11; 4, 11; 189, 2 and elsewh. In the latter case it may also be rendered by or (Lat. sive). After an enumeration of several things, it serves to point back, or to comprise: ža, za, a, ya, ša, sa-ste drug - ni (zha, za, 'a, ya, sha, sa ste drug ni) the six letters ž (zha) etc.; gsum na-ro kyi-gu greṅ-bu-ste (gsum na ro kyi gu greng bu ste) three signs, o, i, and e Glr., Tar. 188, 16; da-ste žag bdun-na (da ste zhag bdun na) as to the being now, in seven days, i.e. in seven days from to-day Dzl.; sometimes ste (ste) seems to stand in the place of a preceding verb, Feer Introd. 73, s.l.c.; at other times it is used, where its exact meaning is not obvious.

or steu (ste'u), carpenter's axe, adz, an axe with its blade athwart the handle (Cs.: 'paring axe'), used by Indian and Tibetan carpenters, Hind. basūla, ste-ltag (ste ltag) its back, ste-yu (ste yu) its handle, ste-k'a (ste kha) Cs. its edge, though in S.g. 32 ste-k'a so-yñyis-pa (ste kha so gnyis pa) it must be the name of the tool itself. -- ste bzog gtoṅ-ba (ste bzog gtong ba) to pare, to smooth, to hew with the axe. -- *pag-ste* (*pag ste*) W. a plane.

also stegs-bu (stegs bu), any contrivance for putting things on, a stand, board, table, stool etc.; ka-stegs (ka stegs) the pedestal or base of a pillar Cs.; rkaṅ-stegs (rkang stegs) foot-stool, jack, horse (wooden frame with legs); *kyoṅ-stag* (*kyong stag*) W. candlestick; *čos-stag* (*chos stag*); *čo-tag* (*cho tag*) W., book-stand; °dug-stegs ('dug stegs) a board, stool, bench, to sit on Cs.; *do-teg* (*do teg*) C. a stone-seat, whether artificial or natural; snod-stegs (snod stegs) Cs. 'a board to put vessels on'; p'or-stegs (phor stegs) a cupboard Cs.; *p'o-stag* (*pho stag*) W. a bench; žabs-stegs (zhabs stegs) resp. for rkaṅ-stegs (rkang stegs); *žuṅ-teg* (*zhung teg*) C. candlestick; gžug-stegs (gzhug stegs) a board to place things on Cs.; za-stegs (za stegs) dining-table Schr.; gsol-stegs (gsol stegs) id resp., and table in general, col. *sol-tag* (*sol tag*); lam-stegs (lam stegs) seat, resting-place by the road-side Glr.; *°oṅ-teg* (*'ong teg*) C. candlestick.
